lede: A look at October 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Brian Branch and Byul, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Same-sex marriage in Northern Ireland
events.desc: Same-sex marriage is legalised, and abortion is decriminalised in Northern Ireland as a result of the Northern Ireland Assembly not being restored.

events.title: Bellview Airlines Flight 210
events.desc: Bellview Airlines Flight 210 crashes in Nigeria, killing all 117 people on board.

events.title: Space Shuttle Columbia
events.desc: Space Shuttle Columbia launches on STS-52 to deploy the LAGEOS-2 satellite and microgravity experiments.

events.title: John Adams (composer)
events.desc: John Adams' opera Nixon in China premiered at the Houston Grand Opera.

events.title: Dod Orsborne
events.desc: Dod Orsborne, captain of the Girl Pat is convicted of its theft and imprisoned, having caused a media sensation when it went missing.

events.title: Leonardopoulos–Gargalidis coup attempt
events.desc: The royalist Leonardopoulos–Gargalidis coup d'état attempt fails in Greece, discrediting the monarchy and paving the way for the establishment of the Second Hellenic Republic.

events.title: Blantyre mining disaster
events.desc: The Blantyre mining disaster in Scotland kills 207 miners.

events.title: André-Jacques Garnerin
events.desc: André-Jacques Garnerin makes the first recorded parachute jump, from 1,000 metres (3,300 ft) above Paris.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of Schmücke dich, o liebe Seele (Adorn yourself, O dear soul) in Leipzig on the 20th Sunday after Trinity, based on the communion hymn of the same name.

events.title: Ahmad ibn Kayghalagh
events.desc: Abbasid general Ahmad ibn Kayghalagh leads a raid against the Byzantine Empire, taking 4,000–5,000 captives.

deaths.text: Richard A. Cash, American global health researcher
Ewan MacColl, English singer-songwriter, producer, actor, and playwright
Joseph Cahill, Australian politician, 29th Premier of New South Wales

observances.text: Christian feast day: Pope John Paul II
